headline: Politics class: Thousands of farmers protest in London against tax changes
body_text:
This article picked by a teacher with suggested questions is part of the Financial Times free schools access programme. Details/registration here.

Read our full range of politics picks here.

Thousands of farmers descended on Westminster to protest against the Labour government’s plans to levy inheritance tax at 20 per cent on farms worth over £1mn. This has angered farmers who argue that in many cases, family farms would have to be sold to pay the tax, from which they were previously exempt.

This is an interesting example of pressure group methods, with the National Farmers’ Union resorting to ‘outsider’ tactics — staging mass demonstrations — whilst continuing their traditional ‘insider’ methods by lobbying MPs in private. Note also the presence of opposition party leaders (Kemi Badenoch and Sir Ed Davey) and celebrity figures (Jeremy Clarkson) in support of the rally.

Click the link below to read the article and then answer the questions:
